snapshot.stmt: For every admissible pair satisfying \(3s_1\alpha<d\), there are fixed admissible choices of \(c_h,c_a,c_N\) and \(\varepsilon\in(0,1-2\gamma)\) such that \(\chi^2(\overline Q_n,(P_0^\circ)^n)\le\exp\{K n^2N_na_n^4h_n^{2d}\}-1\le\varepsilon^2\) eventually.  Hence every \((C_n)_n\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) obeys \(\liminf_{n\to\infty}E_{(P_0^\circ)^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n)]/\delta_n\ge c_{\gamma,\varepsilon}>0\).  Since \(3s_1\alpha<d\) gives \(\xi<1/2\), union honesty forces a strictly slower-than-root-\(n\) expected length at an exact-tie law even though \(\mathcal P_0\) alone admits \(O(n^{-1/2})\) length.

defs.def:admissible-pair: Write \(s=r+\tau\) with \(r=\lfloor s\rfloor\) and \(\tau\in[0,1)\); \(C^s(\mathcal X)\) has bounded continuous derivatives through order \(r\), with order-\(r\) derivatives \(\tau\)-Hölder when \(\tau>0\). Fix \(0<s_1<s_0\), \(L_0,L_1>0\), and \(\alpha,C>0\) satisfying \(s_1\alpha<1\), and choose \(\beta\in(s_1,1/\alpha)\).
defs.def:base-law: Let \(X\sim\operatorname{Unif}(\mathcal X)\), let \(A\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(1/2)\) independently, and conditionally independently draw \(Y(0),Y(1)\in\{-B,B\}\) with conditional means zero.  Let \(P_0^\circ\) be its observed image.
defs.def:sparse-near-tie-family: For each sufficiently large \(n\), use \(\mathcal C_n\) and \(\Delta_{\theta,n}\). Let \(X\sim\operatorname{Unif}(\mathcal X)\), let \(A\sim\operatorname{Bernoulli}(1/2)\) independently, set \(E_F[Y(1)\mid X=x]=\Delta_{\theta,n}(x)/2\) and \(E_F[Y(0)\mid X=x]=-\Delta_{\theta,n}(x)/2\), and conditionally independently draw \(Y(a)\in\{-B,B\}\) with the corresponding Rademacher probabilities.  The constants \(c_h,c_a,c_N\) are fixed small enough that every displayed mean lies in \([-\bar B,\bar B]\), every conditional variance is at least \(v_0\), and the Hölder and margin bounds hold.
defs.def:sequence-frontier: For every positive deterministic \(a=(a_n)_n\), use \(\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) and \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)\) exactly as defined in the symbol table; honesty is a property of the entire interval sequence.

snapshot.stmt:
Let \(\eta=s_1(\alpha+1)/(2s_1+d)\).  There is an explicit split-sample local-polynomial interval sequence \((C_n^\dagger)_n\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) such that
\[
 \sup_{P\in\mathcal P_j}E_{P^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n^\dagger)]
 \le K_\gamma\{n^{-1/2}+n^{-\eta}\},
 \qquad j\in\{0,1\}.
\]
The same interval is uniformly honest on the near-tie stratum.  For every admissible pair, \(s_1\alpha<d\) and hence \(\eta<\xi\); consequently this proved generic upper bound does not attain the sparse lower scale \(\delta_n\asymp n^{-\xi}\).

defs.def:admissible-pair: Write \(s=r+\tau\) with \(r=\lfloor s\rfloor\) and \(\tau\in[0,1)\); \(C^s(\mathcal X)\) has bounded continuous derivatives through order \(r\), with order-\(r\) derivatives \(\tau\)-Hölder when \(\tau>0\). Fix \(0<s_1<s_0\), \(L_0,L_1>0\), and \(\alpha,C>0\) satisfying \(s_1\alpha<1\), and choose \(\beta\in(s_1,1/\alpha)\).
defs.def:sequence-frontier: For every positive deterministic \(a=(a_n)_n\), use \(\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) and \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)\) exactly as defined in the symbol table; honesty is a property of the entire interval sequence.
defs.def:union-upper-route: Construct a connected interval by fitting multiscale contrast estimates on training folds, using independent holdout folds to form simultaneous confidence bounds for their dyadic block coefficients, and calibrating the final interval to the maximum smoothing and nuisance bias over both \(\mathcal F_0\) and \(\mathcal F_1\).

snapshot.stmt:
Let \(\eta=s_1(\alpha+1)/(2s_1+d)\).  For both \(j\in\{0,1\}\), the honest expected-length frontier has the following proved phase diagram.  If \(3s_1\alpha<d\), then
\[
 0<\mathfrak R_j^{\mathrm{asy}}((n^{-\xi})_n)
 \quad\text{and}\quad
 \mathfrak R_j^{\mathrm{asy}}((n^{-\eta})_n)<\infty.
\]
If \(3s_1\alpha\ge d\), then
\[
 0<\mathfrak R_j^{\mathrm{asy}}((n^{-1/2})_n)
 \quad\text{and}\quad
 \mathfrak R_j^{\mathrm{asy}}((n^{-\min\{1/2,\eta\}})_n)<\infty.
\]
In particular, when \(2s_1\alpha\ge d\), a root-\(n\) union-honest connected interval is attainable and the root-\(n\) order is minimax.  In the regimes \(3s_1\alpha<d\) and \(d/3\le s_1\alpha<d/2\), respectively, the proved lower and upper exponents do not coincide, so no matching-frontier conclusion follows from these bounds.

defs.def:admissible-pair: Write \(s=r+\tau\) with \(r=\lfloor s\rfloor\) and \(\tau\in[0,1)\); \(C^s(\mathcal X)\) has bounded continuous derivatives through order \(r\), with order-\(r\) derivatives \(\tau\)-Hölder when \(\tau>0\). Fix \(0<s_1<s_0\), \(L_0,L_1>0\), and \(\alpha,C>0\) satisfying \(s_1\alpha<1\), and choose \(\beta\in(s_1,1/\alpha)\).
defs.def:sequence-frontier: For every positive deterministic \(a=(a_n)_n\), use \(\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) and \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)\) exactly as defined in the symbol table; honesty is a property of the entire interval sequence.

snapshot.stmt: Does the union-calibrated route in \(\mathrm{def:union-upper-route}\) yield a sequence \((C_n^\dagger)_n\in\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) with \(\sup_{P\in\mathcal P_0}E_{P^n}[\operatorname{length}(C_n^\dagger)]=O(\delta_n)\) and the corresponding near-tie risk controlled on \(\mathcal P_1\)?

defs.def:sequence-frontier: For every positive deterministic \(a=(a_n)_n\), use \(\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) and \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)\) exactly as defined in the symbol table; honesty is a property of the entire interval sequence.
defs.def:union-upper-route: Construct a connected interval by fitting multiscale contrast estimates on training folds, using independent holdout folds to form simultaneous confidence bounds for their dyadic block coefficients, and calibrating the final interval to the maximum smoothing and nuisance bias over both \(\mathcal F_0\) and \(\mathcal F_1\).

snapshot.stmt: Since \(\xi-1/2=(3s_1\alpha-d)/[2(d+s_1\alpha+4s_1)]\), the displayed sparse family has \(\delta_n\asymp n^{-\xi}\), equal to root-\(n\) order when \(3s_1\alpha=d\) and \(o(n^{-1/2})\) when \(3s_1\alpha>d\). Is a root-\(n\) union-honest connected interval attainable when \(3s_1\alpha\ge d\), or does a different least-favorable family establish a binding obstruction there? When \(3s_1\alpha<d\), can a union-honest interval attain the proved \(n^{-\xi}\) lower scale?

defs.def:admissible-pair: Write \(s=r+\tau\) with \(r=\lfloor s\rfloor\) and \(\tau\in[0,1)\); \(C^s(\mathcal X)\) has bounded continuous derivatives through order \(r\), with order-\(r\) derivatives \(\tau\)-Hölder when \(\tau>0\). Fix \(0<s_1<s_0\), \(L_0,L_1>0\), and \(\alpha,C>0\) satisfying \(s_1\alpha<1\), and choose \(\beta\in(s_1,1/\alpha)\).
defs.def:sequence-frontier: For every positive deterministic \(a=(a_n)_n\), use \(\mathfrak C^{\mathrm{asy}}(\mathcal U,\gamma)\) and \(\mathfrak R^{\mathrm{asy}}_j(a)\) exactly as defined in the symbol table; honesty is a property of the entire interval sequence.
